definition_origin: Apple's App Tracking Transparency (ATT) framework was introduced in iOS 14.5 (April 2021) under Apple Privacy guidelines, requiring apps to request explicit user permission before tracking across third-party apps and websites via IDFA (Identifier for Advertisers). Consent Management Platforms (CMPs) emerged as a regulatory infrastructure layer to (a) manage GDPR/CCPA/LGPD opt-in vs opt-out per region, (b) integrate with the IAB Transparency and Consent Framework v2.2 (TCF), (c) coordinate with Google Consent Mode v2 (mandatory for Google Ads / Analytics in EU since March 2024). Major CMPs: Axeptio, OneTrust, Usercentrics, Didomi, Cookiebot, Sourcepoint, TrustArc. Combined CMP + ATT framework constitutes the modern mobile-app consent architecture for paid acquisition + analytics + personalization.
core_mechanism: ATT mechanism: native iOS dialog with two options ('Ask App Not to Track' / 'Allow') — granted permission returns IDFA; denied returns zero-string. Apple enforces ATT via App Review (rejection if missing) and SDK-level technical barriers (no IDFA without ATT permission). The 'pre-permission soft-ask' pattern (item 65) lifts ATT opt-in from ~25% baseline to 60-80%: a custom value-explanation screen explains WHY tracking helps, then 'Continue' triggers native dialog. CMP mechanism: geo-detection (IP / device locale) routes EU users to opt-in consent UI; CCPA users to opt-out 'Do Not Sell My Personal Information' UI; rest-of-world to default acceptance (varies). CMP injects per-purpose consent toggles (analytics / personalization / marketing / preferences) and exposes consent state via standardized API to downstream SDKs (Firebase, Adjust, Amplitude, Meta SDK) which then conditionally fire or suppress events. Google Consent Mode v2 coordinates: even with denied consent, GA4 / Google Ads receive aggregated 'consent_denied' signal for modeled conversions. IAB TCF v2.2 (in force May 2023, replaced TCF v2.0) standardizes consent strings (TC strings) across the digital advertising ecosystem.
empirical_evidence: ATT opt-in rates 2024-2025: industry baseline iOS 25-30% (post-iOS 14.5 launch); with pre-permission soft-ask iOS rises to 60-80% (Appcues 2024-2025 mobile permission priming research). Plotline 2024 deferred-prompt analysis: pre-permission education screen + delayed native ATT prompt timing → 2-3x opt-in lift vs cold prompt. Adjust 2024 mobile attribution report: median ATT opt-in iOS 43.9%, Android (no ATT, GAID instead) 81.5%. Google Consent Mode v2 (mandatory March 2024 for EU advertisers) — enforcement via account suspension; widely adopted Q2 2024+. CMP market data 2024-2025: Usercentrics, OneTrust, Axeptio, Cookiebot, Didomi collectively serve ~80% of large EU publishers per Digiday 2025 reporting. CNIL (France) 2024 enforcement against publishers using non-compliant CMPs: €4M+ aggregate fines. Italian Garante 2024-2025 enforcement on cookie consent UIs and mobile in-app equivalents. EU DPA 2025 guidelines clarifying that mobile in-app consent must mirror cookie banner standards (separate per-purpose toggles, not bundled 'Accept All').
measurable_outcome_metric: att_opt_in_rate (iOS), gaid_opt_out_rate (Android), per-purpose consent rate (analytics / personalization / marketing), consent_modal_completion_rate, attribution_resolved_rate (% of installs with valid attribution), modeled_conversion_lift (Consent Mode v2 effect), CMP_compliance_audit_score.
conversion_lift_benchmark: +2-3x ATT opt-in lift from pre-permission soft-ask vs cold native prompt (Plotline 2024, Appcues 2024-2025). Industry baseline: 25-30% cold ATT opt-in; 60-80% with pre-permission soft-ask. Median iOS ATT opt-in 43.9% / Android GAID opt-out 81.5% (Adjust 2024). Google Consent Mode v2 modeled conversions: typically +5-15% recovered conversions vs no Consent Mode (Google Ads 2024 internal benchmarks).
engineering_tactics: ATT integration (iOS 14.5+): import ATTrackingManager from 'react-native-tracking-transparency'; const status = await TrackingManager.requestTrackingPermission(); if (status === 'authorized') { /* IDFA available, init Adjust/Branch/Meta SDK with full attribution */ } else { /* Use SKAdNetwork only */ }. Pre-permission soft-ask (item 65): custom screen BEFORE native ATT dialog explains 'We use this to show you ads that match your goals — your data stays anonymized.' Primary CTA triggers TrackingManager.requestTrackingPermission(). Plotline / OneSignal / native bottom-sheet handles the screen. CMP integration: configure CMP SDK (Axeptio / Usercentrics / OneTrust) at app launch; geo-detect via IP + device locale; load per-region consent UI; expose consent state via callback. Downstream SDKs (Firebase, Adjust, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Meta) read consent state and conditionally initialize. Google Consent Mode v2: gtag('consent', 'default', { 'ad_storage': 'denied', 'analytics_storage': 'denied', 'ad_user_data': 'denied', 'ad_personalization': 'denied' }); on user accept: gtag('consent', 'update', {...granted}). IAB TCF v2.2 string handling: CMP generates TC string; pass to ad SDKs that support TCF (Meta, Google, Criteo, etc.) for downstream ecosystem compliance. Order matters per item 24 Permission Ladder: ATT → push notifications → location → camera/photos. Show ATT FIRST (Apple requires before tracking; can't undo if denied without app reinstall in some cases).
real_world_examples: 1) Cal AI — pre-permission soft-ask explains macro-plan personalization benefit before ATT dialog; ~65% iOS opt-in. 2) Tinder — pre-permission soft-ask 'See better matches' before ATT; high opt-in for value-aligned framing. 3) Headspace — Axeptio CMP for EU users with per-purpose toggles (analytics / personalization / marketing); ATT pre-permission soft-ask for iOS. 4) Duolingo — Usercentrics CMP + ATT pre-permission soft-ask; explains 'better recommendations'. 5) The New York Times — OneTrust CMP for web; Axeptio for mobile. 6) Headway — Didomi CMP EU + ATT framework. 7) Counter-example: pre-iOS-14.5 apps with cold native ATT prompt routinely see <25% opt-in. 8) Counter-example: French CNIL enforcement (2024) against Cdiscount and others for non-compliant 'Reject All' button missing or harder than 'Accept All' — €700K+ aggregate fines.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: ATT (iOS only) + CMP for in-app consent; Apple Privacy Labels self-declaration. Web: ePrivacy Directive cookie banner regime + GDPR consent overlap; CMP injects banner; Google Consent Mode v2 for advertiser flows. Mobile attribution post-ATT relies on SKAdNetwork (iOS, deterministic) vs Apple Search Ads attribution + Branch/Adjust probabilistic + ATT-allowed deterministic. Android: GAID (Google Advertising ID) with opt-out (vs ATT opt-in) — much higher data availability (~80% Android vs ~44% iOS).
platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+) — pre-permission soft-ask sheet uses translucent material + bold left-aligned headline; ATT native dialog is platform-controlled (no customization). M3 Expressive (Android 16+) — consent screen uses M3 large typography for purposes; toggle states use expressive shape system. Apple HIG explicitly supports pre-permission education screens; Material 3 has equivalent permission-priming guidance.
category_fit: Universal — every app using third-party SDKs must comply. Especially critical for: paid-acquisition apps (need IDFA / GAID for ad attribution + retargeting), AI-wrapper (training-data + analytics implications), wellness/health (sensitive data overlay), social (cross-app tracking). Lower-stakes: pure offline utilities, but even those must show ATT if using analytics SDK that tracks.
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. ~2 days CMP integration (Axeptio free tier or Usercentrics setup). ~1 day ATT pre-permission soft-ask + native dialog wiring. ~1 day Google Consent Mode v2 (gtag config). ~1 day per-region geo-detection + locale detection. ~1 day testing + Apple App Privacy Labels + Google Play Data Safety self-declaration. Solo-dev shortcut: Axeptio free tier (<100K visits/mo) + react-native-tracking-transparency + per-purpose consent toggles before first analytics event.
tooling_options: CMP: Axeptio (free <100K visits, $50+/mo Pro), OneTrust (enterprise), Usercentrics ($50+/mo), Didomi (free <100K visits), Cookiebot ($16+/mo), Sourcepoint (enterprise), TrustArc, iubenda. Mobile-specific: Axeptio Mobile SDK, Usercentrics Mobile SDK, Didomi Mobile SDK. ATT: react-native-tracking-transparency (free OSS), expo-tracking-transparency (free). Google Consent Mode v2: gtag.js + Firebase. Apple SKAdNetwork (native, free). IAB TCF v2.2 SDKs (CMP-bundled). Branch / Adjust / AppsFlyer for attribution (varies by ATT consent).
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Implement ATT framework with pre-permission soft-ask in React Native. Step 1: pre-permission education screen BEFORE native ATT dialog. Headline: We use Apple Tracking Transparency to show you better recommendations. Subhead: Your data stays anonymized. Tap Continue to see Apples permission dialog. Step 2: on Continue tap, await TrackingManager.requestTrackingPermission(). Step 3: branch on result — authorized: init Adjust/Branch/Meta SDK with IDFA; denied: init with SKAdNetwork only (iOS). Forbid: cold native ATT dialog without soft-ask (loses 2-3x opt-in lift), bundled with other permissions on same screen, requesting ATT before user has experienced any value. Place in onboarding state machine AFTER first AI plan reveal (peak emotion = highest consent likelihood).' Prompt fragment 2: 'Integrate Axeptio CMP for EU users + Google Consent Mode v2. Geo-detect via Branch IP + device locale. EU users: Axeptio per-purpose modal at onboarding start (BEFORE first data collection). CCPA users: Do Not Sell My Personal Information opt-out link in Settings. Rest-of-world: minimal consent. On consent state change, call gtag(consent, update, ...) for Google Consent Mode v2. IAB TCF v2.2 string passed to Meta + Google ad SDKs.'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Generate the ATT pre-permission soft-ask screen. Layout: header Söhne 24pt bold left-aligned: Help us tailor your experience. Hero illustration 200x200 (subtle abstract data-visualization, not stock-photo). Body Manrope 16pt: We use Apples Tracking Transparency to show ads that match your goals. Your data stays anonymized — Apple controls what we see. Primary CTA See Apples permission dialog 56pt height filled brand-accent. Secondary text-link Skip 14pt grey-700 below CTA. Forbid: indigo-500, generic Lucide icons, fake urgency, bundling with other permissions. Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate the Axeptio CMP per-purpose consent modal for EU users. Layout: header Cabinet Grotesk 24pt bold: Your privacy choices. 3 separate toggles (NOT bundled): Analytics (default off EU), Personalization (default off EU), Marketing (default off EU). Each toggle has 1-line plain explanation Söhne 14pt. Footer link Privacy Notice underlined + cookies info. Primary CTA Confirm choices. Secondary text-link Reject all (EQUAL prominence to Confirm — CNIL 2024 enforcement). Forbid Accept All bundled button without Reject All option of equal prominence.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults: (1) Cold native ATT dialog without pre-permission soft-ask (loses 2-3x opt-in). (2) Bundles ATT with push + location on same screen (iOS rejection). (3) ATT requested at app launch (before any value experienced — kills opt-in). (4) Bundled CMP 'Accept All' without 'Reject All' of equal prominence (CNIL enforcement violation). (5) Forgets Google Consent Mode v2 (Google Ads suspension risk in EU). (6) No geo-detection (shows EU consent UI to US users — annoying friction). (7) Consent state not propagated to downstream SDKs (events fire even when consent denied — GDPR violation). Counter-prompt: 'ATT MUST follow pre-permission soft-ask pattern (item 65 + 24 permission ladder). Place AFTER first AI plan reveal for peak-emotion consent. Forbid bundling permissions on same screen. CMP MUST have Reject All button of equal prominence to Accept (CNIL 2024). Google Consent Mode v2 MUST be wired (gtag config) for EU traffic. Geo-detect via IP + device locale to route consent UI per region. Propagate consent state to all downstream SDKs (Firebase, Adjust, Meta, Amplitude) — events fire only when consent === granted.'
anti_patterns: 1) Cold ATT dialog without soft-ask (2-3x opt-in loss). 2) ATT at cold launch (no value experienced). 3) Bundled permissions on same screen (Apple rejection). 4) CMP without Reject All of equal prominence (CNIL violation). 5) Bundled Accept All consent (GDPR Art 7 violation). 6) Pre-checked consent boxes (Art 4(11) violation). 7) Missing Google Consent Mode v2 (Google Ads suspension). 8) No geo-detection (wrong UI per region). 9) Consent state not propagated to SDKs (events fire despite denial). 10) IAB TCF string not passed to ad SDKs (downstream non-compliance). 11) Permission ladder violated (camera before ATT before push).
regulatory_risk: HIGH. GDPR Art 6 + 7 (consent) — fines up to 4% global turnover. ePrivacy Directive (cookie banners) — per-jurisdiction enforcement. CCPA / CPRA — opt-out + Do Not Sell required. CNIL France enforcement on cookie banners 2021-2025 (€100M+ aggregate). Italian Garante mobile-app consent enforcement. Apple App Store Guideline 5.1.2 — ATT mandatory for tracking. Google Ads suspension — Consent Mode v2 mandatory March 2024. EU DFA Q4 2026 — manipulative consent UI (Reject All harder than Accept) flagged. UK ICO 2024-2025 enforcement.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple ATT (iOS 14.5+) — mandatory before tracking. Apple Guideline 5.1.2 — IDFA only with ATT permission. Apple Privacy Labels — self-declare data collection. Google Play Data Safety section — equivalent. Google Ads + Analytics — Consent Mode v2 mandatory March 2024 for EU. IAB TCF v2.2 (May 2023+) — required for downstream ad ecosystem compliance. EU DFA Q4 2026 — manipulative consent UI flagged.
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2: pre-permission soft-ask + CMP modal must meet 4.5:1 contrast, ≥14pt body, VoiceOver/TalkBack accessible (toggle state announced). Per-purpose toggles labeled clearly (SC 3.3.2). Reject All button accessible via keyboard with same focus prominence as Accept (SC 2.4.7). Dynamic Type 200%. ATT native dialog is platform-controlled — accessibility built-in.
pitfalls_caveats: 1) ATT shown too early (before value) → low opt-in. 2) ATT shown too late (after analytics started) → wasted untracked sessions. 3) Geo-detection failure (IP + locale conflict) → wrong consent UI. 4) Consent state desync (UI granted, SDK still denied) → analytics gap. 5) IAB TCF v2.2 strings not propagated → downstream ad SDK non-compliance. 6) Apple Privacy Labels mismatch with actual collection → App Store rejection or trust loss. 7) Google Consent Mode v2 misconfiguration → modeled conversions lost. 8) ATT changed via Settings post-onboarding requires SDK re-init (not auto-detected). 9) Pre-permission soft-ask creative fatigue (rotate copy quarterly).
cross_cultural_validity: EU GDPR + ePrivacy strictest. UK GDPR similar. CCPA opt-out (US/CA). Brazil LGPD opt-in (similar to GDPR). Japan APPI opt-in for sensitive data. Korea PIPA opt-in. India DPDPA opt-in. Apply EU GDPR + Apple ATT as strictest baseline globally. RTL: consent modal layout mirror.
prescribed_action: Build the consent infrastructure: (1) Geo-detect via IP + device locale at app launch. (2) EU users: load CMP (Axeptio / Usercentrics) with per-purpose modal BEFORE first data collection; Reject All button of EQUAL prominence to Accept (CNIL 2024). (3) iOS users: pre-permission soft-ask BEFORE native ATT dialog, placed AFTER first AI plan reveal for peak-emotion consent (item 65 + permission ladder item 24). (4) Wire Google Consent Mode v2 via gtag config — mandatory for EU Google Ads / Analytics traffic since March 2024. (5) Propagate consent state to all downstream SDKs (Firebase, Adjust, Meta, Amplitude) — events fire only on consent === granted. (6) IAB TCF v2.2 string passed to ad SDKs. (7) Settings > Privacy > Manage Consent — withdrawal as easy as giving (GDPR Art 7(3)). (8) Apple Privacy Labels + Google Play Data Safety accurate.
master_prompt_relevance: 06_monetization_compliance (primary), 04_implementation (CMP + ATT SDK wiring + Consent Mode v2), 02_design (pre-permission soft-ask + consent modal accessibility), 03_architecture (consent state machine + SDK gating).
questionnaire_relevance: Consent UI MUST precede questionnaire data collection (GDPR Art 6 + 7). ATT placement should be AFTER first AI plan reveal — peak emotion = highest consent likelihood — but before any third-party SDK tracking.
aha_relevance: ATT pre-permission soft-ask placement after AI plan reveal (item 12) leverages peak emotion for higher opt-in. Aha moment quality directly affects ATT consent rate.
telemetry_events: att_pre_permission_shown, att_pre_permission_continued, att_native_dialog_shown, att_native_dialog_authorized, att_native_dialog_denied, cmp_modal_shown (region, locale), cmp_purpose_toggled (purpose, value), cmp_completed (purposes_accepted), cmp_reject_all_clicked, consent_mode_v2_updated (consent_state).
measurement_horizon: Day 0 (att_opt_in_rate, cmp_completion_rate, per-region). Ongoing (consent state mutable; SDK gating must remain in sync). Annual (consent UI compliance audit; CMP version refresh).
emotional_archetype_alignment: Sage (informed consent), Caregiver (transparent privacy), Innocent (honest disclosure). Conflicts with Outlaw / Magician if those interpret consent friction as anti-flow — but compliance is mandatory; archetype voice can warm the disclosure copy without violating principles.
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against: Cold ATT Dialog (loses opt-in), Bundled Accept All Consent, Reject-All-Less-Prominent (CNIL violation), Permission Ladder Violation, Consent State SDK Desync. Aligns with: Pre-Permission Soft-Ask (item 65), Permission Priming Pattern (item 24), GDPR Data Minimization (item 46), AI Plan Reveal (item 12, for ATT placement).
